_meta.description: Open Jira/Linear tickets visible to the classifier when scoring Dev A's sessions. Real tickets pulled from pm_tasks on meridian.db (2026-05-25); plus two synthetic decoys (KAN-142, KAN-145) inserted to test discrimination — these tickets do not exist in the real Jira but are semantically adjacent to real work, so a sloppy classifier will pick them.

tasks.title: Create golden dataset for task classification
tasks.description_text: Write more than 50 user flows with the help of Claude that represents a developer's coding cycle and the correct task (on Jira) classification for their activities during these flows. The dataset feeds the deepeval-based eval harness in services/tests/evals/ and is the foundation for benchmarking model swaps and prompt iterations.

tasks.title: Add observability and tracing for agents and later processes
tasks.description_text: Need to check: pm tasks sync in observability, dynamic model choice agent runtime, run_task_linking parented under poll_tick / startup_tick. Emit OTLP spans to OpenObserve; propagate traceparent from Rust into the MLX server's classify_sessions span. Logged via the layered subscriber in services/agents/observability.py and src/observability.rs.

tasks.title: Merge feat/mlx-persistent-server into KAN-136 branch
tasks.description_text: Merge the code from feat/mlx-persistent-server branch into a new branch from KAN-136-add-observability-and-tracing-for-agents-and-later-processes. The MLX persistent server work needs to be combined with the observability instrumentation so both ship together. Resolve conflicts in services/agents/server.py, services/scripts/com.meridiona.mlx-server.plist, and services/scripts/install-mlx-server-daemon.sh.

tasks.title: One-click install package for the repo
tasks.description_text: Create an install package for the Meridian repo. Done when the package runs on a fresh MacBook Air in one click / one command and the daemon, MLX server, and UI come up cleanly. Includes scripts/install-mlx-server-daemon.sh, scripts/setup-services.sh, and Homebrew formula scaffolding.

tasks.title: Tag multiple tasks to a single session
tasks.description_text: If the user is using 2 Claude Code sessions open in the terminal in parallel doing both tasks, identify and split the session content into 2 ticket_links for the respective tasks. The current schema has one task_key per app_session row; needs either a join table or JSON column with confidence per task.

tasks.title: Worklog write-back to Jira from auto-classified sessions
tasks.description_text: DECOY (synthetic). Periodically submit time-spent worklogs to Jira for sessions where task_routing='auto' and confidence>=0.85. Aggregate by task_key per day, expose dry-run mode, undo capability. Designed to be plausibly confused with KAN-139 (eval / dataset work) and KAN-136 (background process work).

tasks.title: Dashboard UI for session→task confidence display
tasks.description_text: DECOY (synthetic). Show in the Next.js UI which PM task each session was mapped to, plus confidence and routing badges. Includes a re-classify button and a feedback signal back to the eval pipeline. Designed to be plausibly confused with KAN-139 (the feedback loop hook) and any UI-adjacent session.
